question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the enabled flag being a simple boolean with no integrity protection to flip it via a configuration injection attack (e.g., environment variable override), disabling Hashgraph history ingestion and creating a gap that allows tampered history to be inserted when re-enabled?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxSize field minimum of 2 bytes in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) to craft a configuration where maxSize is set just above the minimum, causing all real stream files to be rejected as too large while only attacker-crafted minimal files are accepted, corrupting Hashgraph history?
